BLOCKING (DISS-001): config.mibera.yaml handler entrypoint vs scoped codegen
description: Cross-model dissent (codex-headless) during /review-sprint sprint-1. config.mibera.yaml points both belt contracts at handler: src/EventHandlers.ts (verbatim per SDD 3.1). src/EventHandlers.ts is the monolith barrel — statically imports all 31 handler modules; each imports its contract namespace from generated. A scoped envio codegen --config config.mibera.yaml generates generated/ for only the 2 belt contracts, so the other 39 contracts' handler modules fail to resolve generated imports → build breaks before the belt indexer runs. SDD-level gap: SDD 3.2 'handlers reused as-is, zero code changes' + sprint.md:22 'No handler code changes anywhere' are incompatible with a scoped config + monolith barrel entrypoint. Fix candidate: per-belt handler entrypoint src/EventHandlers.mibera.ts (additive). Needs operator/architecture decision — escalated; run HALTED. See grimoires/loa/a2a/sprint-1/engineer-feedback.md.
close_reason: DISS-001 resolved (cycle 3): belt entrypoint relocated to src/belts/mibera/EventHandlers.mibera.ts; config.mibera.yaml sets top-level handlers: src/belts/mibera. Envio autoload glob ./src/belts/mibera/**/*.{js,mjs,ts} verified to resolve to only the belt entrypoint. verify gate exit 0, 10/10 tests, tsc-clean.

comments.author: review-sprint
comments.text: RESOLUTION (operator, 2026-05-20): Option 1 — fix now. Add src/EventHandlers.mibera.ts (belt-scoped registration entrypoint, 2 Mibera handlers only); repoint config.mibera.yaml handler: fields to it; refine verify-belt-config.js + test to compare name+events+field_selection excluding the handler: line (SDD 5.3 scope). Cycle-2 /implement addresses it. See engineer-feedback.md RESOLUTION.

comments.author: review-sprint
comments.text: REOPENED — cycle-2 re-review: belt entrypoint is necessary but insufficient. Verified vs node_modules/envio/src/HandlerLoader.res.mjs: registerAllHandlers() calls autoLoadFromSrcHandlers(config.handlers) UNCONDITIONALLY; top-level handlers key defaults to src/handlers (Envio schema). So a scoped codegen still glob-imports all src/handlers/*.ts. Cycle-3 fix: move entrypoint to src/belts/mibera/EventHandlers.mibera.ts + set config.mibera.yaml top-level 'handlers: src/belts/mibera'. See engineer-feedback.md.

title: S2-T3: Local dev run — 3 handler-emission queries (operator-paired)

comments.author: soju
comments.text: S2-T3 BLOCKED -> DISS-003: Envio v3.0.0-alpha.14 RPC source can't parse Berachain blocks (requires totalDifficulty, Berachain omits it). HyperSync works but now needs a (free) ENVIO_API_TOKEN -- likely the original fire. r4's belt->eRPC->RPC premise is broken (eRPC is a transparent proxy, doesn't inject totalDifficulty). Needs architecture decision.

comments.author: soju
comments.text: SALVAGE investigated (2026-05-20): (1) eRPC does NOT inject totalDifficulty (ran eRPC locally, getBlockByNumber passes upstream through unchanged) -- not a feature. (2) Envio alpha.14->3.0.0 stable is breaking: handler-registration API changed, reused handlers register 0 events (eventConfigs=0), can't reach totalDifficulty stage. Reverted. RPC salvage = custom shim OR full Envio migration; neither quick. HyperSync+free token is the simple restore.

comments.author: soju
comments.text: DISS-003 RESOLVED (validated 2026-05-20): bump envio alpha.14->alpha.16 fixes totalDifficulty (Envio #994/#998) AND keeps the handler API (no eventConfigs=0; that break is only at stable 3.0.0). Tested on 2 fresh endpoints: no td error, only per-endpoint rate-limiting (eRPC's job). Sovereign path validated: belt->eRPC->free RPC cluster on alpha.16+. Caveat: alpha.16 npm pkg lacks a bin (pnpm envio fails); pin an alpha with a working bin or invoke platform binary directly.

comments.author: soju
comments.text: DISS-003 FIX APPLIED (2026-05-20): pinned envio 3.0.0-alpha.17 (expert-endorsed A-prime). alpha.17 = PR#998 totalDifficulty fix + alpha.15/16 binary fix (pnpm envio works again, unlike alpha.16) + below alpha.23 handler-API break. Validated: pnpm envio / codegen / typecheck:mibera / verify-belt-config all green; envio start reaches 'Starting indexing' (eventConfigs>0), no totalDifficulty error. S2-T3 unblocked; entity-emission proof via eRPC next.

title: S0-T2 — Measure free-RPC + eRPC cold-sync rate (H1 experiment)
description: Stand eRPC up disposably over the S0-T1 endpoint cluster; cold-sync the Mibera belt from block 3837808; measure blocks/sec, cache behavior, stall/progress. No preset pass/fail. Artifact: grimoires/loa/spikes/s0-erpc-calibration.md.
close_reason: S0-T2 done — eRPC cold-sync measured: getLogs ~39k blk/s cold / ~192k warm (4.9x cache), block-fetch 61ms cold / 3ms warm, 0 rate-limiting. Cold-sync extrapolated minutes-to-low-hours. Encouraging for H1. See grimoires/loa/spikes/s0-erpc-calibration.md.

title: Pre-existing: src/handlers/sf-vaults.ts fails tsc --noEmit
description: tsc --noEmit reports ~7 errors in src/handlers/sf-vaults.ts (TS2322/TS2551 — LatestVaultStrategy not on HandlerContext; strategyAddress object vs string). Surfaced when node_modules was hydrated to pnpm-lock.yaml (stale node_modules had typescript 5.2.2 + mocha; lockfile declares typescript 5.9.3). NOT introduced by indexer-belt-rebuild Sprint 1 — git status src/ is empty, tsc include is [src] only. sf-vaults = S&F Vault handler, an ARCHIVED/dead contract per the build spec. generated/ dir is dated Feb 15 (stale). S2-T3 build gate runs pnpm codegen first — codegen may resolve these if they are stale generated-type artifacts, else they are a real pre-existing handler bug. Out of scope for Sprint 1.

comments.author: implement
comments.text: Correction (cycle 2): tsc --noEmit pre-existing errors span 3 handler files — fatbera.ts, puru-apiculture1155.ts, sf-vaults.ts. Cycle-1 report saw only sf-vaults (tail -10 truncation). All are the same class: handler code imports generated members absent from the stale Feb-15 generated/ (LatestValidatorDeposit, PuruApiculture1155, LatestVaultStrategy, etc.). S2-T3 pnpm codegen regenerates generated/ and should resolve them. Sprint 1's new file src/EventHandlers.mibera.ts is tsc-clean.

title: Project git remote not configured — only loa-upstream (framework repo) present
description: git remote -v shows only: loa-upstream -> https://github.com/0xHoneyJar/loa.git (the Loa FRAMEWORK repo). No origin / no 0xHoneyJar/thj-envio remote. Blocks the /run sprint-1 draft-PR step — run-mode did NOT push or open a PR (pushing to loa-upstream would land the indexer in the framework repo). Sprint 1 work is committed locally on branch indexer-belt-rebuild (8 commits ahead of main). To publish: git remote add origin https://github.com/0xHoneyJar/thj-envio.git (verify URL), then git push -u origin indexer-belt-rebuild, then open the PR. Also note CI .github/workflows/ci.yml is the vestigial Loa-template CI — its template-guard job will fail on this branch (prd.md/sdd.md/sprint.md committed); unrelated to Sprint 1.
close_reason: Remotes configured (origin → 0xHoneyJar/thj-envio/sonar-api, upstream → moose-code/thj). Branch indexer-belt-rebuild pushed; draft PR #13 created.

title: Sense: live grounding logic
description: agreement/cache => grounded; circuit-open => unverifiable; contradiction => refuted; trace_id from Sonar event id
notes:
OPEN FAGAN findings (panel /tmp/fagan-bd-zfj5.json, 3-voice, 8 majors) — RESUME after meta-coherence work:
1. [IMPORTANT] verify block-skew: reads latest from 2 upstreams w/o pinning a common block -> spurious refuted on head skew (L297/302). FIX: pin common block (min head / finalized) before cross-check.
2. [IMPORTANT] verify ignores erpcUrl: uses CHAINS[chain].rpcUrls.slice(0,2) (public only), not rpcUrlsFor() incl eRPC (L295/298). FIX: cross-check over rpcUrlsFor(chain,erpcUrl).
3. crossCheck: a SYNC throw in a read-fn rejects crossCheck itself (L219). FIX: wrap reads (Promise.resolve().then(r)).
4. crossCheck refuted carries value=oks[0] arbitrary (L229) + runRead passes cc.value even when refuted (L304). FIX: refuted -> value undefined or carry both.
5. blockNumberField Number(bigint) precision loss >2^53 (L167) — latent, low-urgency.
